stops.name: Centerville City Park
stops.notes: Shady park near the largest courthouse square in the U.S. Easy, walkable leg-stretch.

stops.name: Judge Lewis Park
stops.notes: In-town park with a paved walking loop and restrooms.

stops.name: Pioneer Park
stops.notes: Right on Highway 2 between Clarinda and Shenandoah. Picnic shelters, restrooms, fishing pond. A classic drive-break spot; also has RV camping with hookups and a dump station.

stops.name: Arbor Lodge State Historical Park
stops.notes: Birthplace of Arbor Day. Large shaded arboretum and picnic grounds. Mansion open Tue-Sun 11am-5pm (grounds open daily).

stops.name: Crete City Park
stops.notes: Right on NE-33. Good midpoint between Nebraska City and Hastings.

stops.name: South Park
stops.notes: Mature shade trees and many picnic tables. Holdrege also has an inexpensive, level city campground for overnighting.

stops.name: George Norris Park
stops.notes: Nicest stop on this leg. Bandshell, big shade trees, restrooms, picnic areas in a real town.

stops.name: Ward Bond Memorial Park
stops.notes: Green and shady with picnic tables and pavilions; pool alongside. Last good Nebraska stop before the state line. Has city RV sites.

stops.name: Yuma City Park
stops.notes: Straightforward town-park break once you are into Colorado.

stops.name: Riverside Park
stops.notes: A genuine oasis on the plains. Riverside nature trails, disc golf, large shaded picnic areas.

stops.name: Viestenz-Smith Mountain Park
stops.notes: Right on US-34 in the canyon. Shaded riverside picnic tables and a CCC-built shelter. Last good pause before the winding climb to Estes Park. Check seasonal opening before relying on it.

stops.name: Howard T. Sawhill City Park
stops.notes: Town-square park with a band shell, oval amphitheater, and central fountain. An easy first stretch-break just inside Kansas after the long run across the eastern Colorado plains.

stops.name: Lake Atwood
stops.notes: Small city lake just north of US-36 with shaded picnic areas and fishing; a quick, walkable break in Atwood.

stops.name: Oberlin City Park
stops.notes: In-town city park right off US-36 — a shaded spot for a quick leg-stretch in the middle of Oberlin.

stops.name: Elmwood Park
stops.notes: Norton's shaded city park with picnic tables and restrooms; a good plains stretch-break.

stops.name: Phillipsburg City Park
stops.notes: Shaded in-town park with picnic tables — a straightforward break in the county seat.

stops.name: Wagner Park
stops.notes: County-seat park near the historic Old Dutch Mill; mature shade trees and picnic tables make a pleasant midpoint stop.

stops.name: Mankato City Park
stops.notes: Quiet county-seat city park for a quick leg-stretch.

stops.name: Belleville City Park
stops.notes: Shaded in-town city park, an easy break along US-36.

stops.name: Washington City Park
stops.notes: City Park with the volunteer-built 'Munchkinland' Wizard-of-Oz playground and three covered picnic shelters with tables — a standout family lunch stop a few blocks north of US-36. (Pinned at the central city office; the park is a short walk away.)

stops.name: Marysville City Park
stops.notes: Home of Marysville's famous black squirrels. Big shaded park with a swimming pool, tennis courts, restrooms, a sod house and an old train engine — the best family stop on this route.

stops.name: Seneca City Park
stops.notes: Shaded city park with shelter houses and restrooms near the west side of town; South Park on the south side also has a picnic shelter and restrooms.

stops.name: Hiawatha City Lake Park
stops.notes: City lake park near the US-36/US-73 junction with picnic areas and fishing; an easy stretch-break.

stops.name: Krug Park
stops.notes: Large historic city park in northwest St. Joseph with a lagoon, gardens, and shaded picnic areas — a good final stop coming into town.
